picks.rationale: The Boston Celtics enter this playoff opener with a clean bill of health and strong recent form, winning 8 of their last 10 games with an impressive +11.2 point differential. Crucially, the Philadelphia 76ers will be without their superstar Joel Embiid, who is officially OUT due to post-appendectomy surgery recovery, a devastating blow to their offense and rebounding. As the No. 2 seed hosting the No. 7 seed, Boston's home-court advantage and overall team strength, combined with Embiid's absence, make covering the -12.5 spread a high-confidence play.

The Oklahoma City Thunder, as the reigning champions and No. 1 seed with a dominant 64-18 regular season record, are in prime position for this Game 1. The Phoenix Suns are facing significant injury concerns, with key players Mark Williams (foot) and Grayson Allen (hamstring) listed as questionable, impacting their depth and spacing. The Thunder, conversely, are largely healthy with only a non-rotation player out for the season. Given OKC's home-court advantage and superior record against a potentially shorthanded Suns team, the -14.5 spread is an attractive pick.

The Detroit Pistons finished as the No. 1 seed in the Eastern Conference with a 60-22 record and are entering the playoffs in excellent form, winning 8 of their last 10 games with a +11.8 point differential. Detroit boasts a clean injury report with Cade Cunningham back and healthy, while the Orlando Magic have Jonathan Isaac questionable with a knee sprain. The Pistons' strengths in forcing turnovers and offensive rebounding could exploit Orlando's recent vulnerabilities in those areas. With their strong home record and full health, Detroit is well-positioned to cover the -8.5 spread.

picks.game: Portland Trail Blazers @ San Antonio Spurs
picks.rationale: The San Antonio Spurs are the No. 2 seed with a 62-20 record and have been dominant recently, winning 8 of their last 10 games with a +13.6 point differential. The Portland Trail Blazers are severely hampered by the season-ending injury to Damian Lillard (torn left Achilles tendon), significantly impacting their offensive firepower. The Spurs, led by Victor Wembanyama, are largely healthy for this playoff opener and hold a strong 32-8 home record. With Lillard out and San Antonio's superior form and home-court advantage, the Spurs are a strong bet to cover the -11 spread.

With Joel Embiid officially ruled OUT for the 76ers due to post-appendectomy surgery recovery, Tyrese Maxey will be forced to shoulder a significantly increased offensive load. Maxey already leads the 76ers in scoring, averaging 28.3 points per game this season. His performance in the recent Play-In tournament, where he scored 31 points, demonstrates his ability to step up in Embiid's absence. Despite a finger strain, he is available, making him a high-confidence pick to exceed 20.5 points.

The Colorado Avalanche enter Game 1 in strong form, winning 4 of their last 5 games, and have dominated the head-to-head against the Kings, winning 4 of the last 5 matchups, including a 4-2 victory on March 2, 2026. Star forward Nathan MacKinnon, who leads the league in goals and points, is confirmed to return to the lineup after resting the season finale, providing a significant offensive boost. The Los Angeles Kings, meanwhile, are heavy underdogs and are without key forward Kevin Fiala for the season, with Andrei Kuzmenko also day-to-day, further weakening their attack. Given Colorado's superior form, H2H dominance, and MacKinnon's return, they are well-positioned to win by multiple goals at home.

The Vegas Golden Knights finished the regular season on an impressive 7-0-1 run, demonstrating strong momentum heading into the playoffs. They possess significant championship pedigree and depth, which provides a crucial situational edge against the Utah Mammoth, who are making their first-ever playoff appearance. While William Karlsson is out for Vegas, goaltender Carter Hart has been exceptional, securing six straight wins with a 1.66 GAA and a .930 save percentage in his recent starts. Furthermore, Vegas holds a clear special teams advantage with the 6th-ranked power play and 7th-ranked penalty kill, compared to Utah's 18th and 19th respectively.

The Buffalo Sabres are significantly hampered by injuries to key offensive players, with Sam Carrick, Josh Norris, and Jordan Greenway all confirmed out for this playoff opener. Carrick, a major trade deadline acquisition, and Norris, a consistent scorer, will be particularly missed in the Sabres' lineup. The Boston Bruins, a veteran team with extensive playoff experience, are well-equipped to exploit Buffalo's depleted forward corps. Despite being the wild card, Boston's experience and the Sabres' substantial injury woes create a valuable betting opportunity on the Bruins at plus money.

Nathan MacKinnon is confirmed to return to the Avalanche lineup for Game 1 after sitting out the season finale, and he is a dominant offensive force, leading the NHL in both goals and points this season. He has averaged an impressive 0.71 goals per game on the road during the 2025-26 campaign. Facing a Kings team that is a heavy underdog and has allowed three goals in each of their last two games, MacKinnon is in an excellent position to find the back of the net. His return from rest, combined with Colorado's potent offense, makes him a strong candidate to score a goal.

The head-to-head history between the Montreal Canadiens and Tampa Bay Lightning suggests a tendency for higher-scoring affairs, with three of their last five matchups exceeding 6.5 goals. Both teams possess significant offensive firepower, with Nikita Kucherov leading the Lightning and Cole Caufield and Juraj Slafkovsky contributing heavily for the Canadiens. While their last two H2H games were lower-scoring, the Canadiens also boast a stronger regular season power play percentage (23.1%) compared to the Lightning (20.7%). This offensive potential from both sides, coupled with the historical H2H trend, points towards a game with more than 6.5 goals.

The San Francisco Giants have a significant pitching advantage with Robbie Ray (2-2, 2.42 ERA) scheduled to start against the Nationals' Miles Mikolas (0-3, 11.49 ERA). The Giants are also in better recent form, holding a 6-4 record in their last 10 games and a three-game road winning streak. In contrast, the Nationals are struggling with a 5-5 record in their last 10 games and a dismal 1-7 home record. This clear pitching mismatch, combined with Washington's poor home performance, makes the Giants a strong pick.

The Houston Astros are in a severe slump, having lost 11 of their last 13 games and holding a 2-8 record in their last 10, including a three-game home losing streak. Their starting pitcher, Mike Burrows, carries a high 6.55 ERA, while the Cardinals' Matthew Liberatore has a more respectable 4.29 ERA. The Cardinals, on the other hand, are hot with a four-game winning streak and a 7-3 record in their last 10 games. Houston also has numerous key pitchers on the injured list, further weakening their staff.

The Milwaukee Brewers are entering this game on a strong four-game winning streak and boast a solid 5-3 away record. Conversely, the Miami Marlins are struggling significantly, currently on a four-game losing streak. The Brewers also secured a 5-2 victory against the Marlins yesterday, indicating recent dominance in the matchup. With the Marlins' continued struggles and the Brewers' strong momentum, Milwaukee is positioned for another win.

picks.game: San Diego Padres @ Los Angeles Angels
picks.rationale: The San Diego Padres are in excellent form, with a 9-1 record in their last 10 games and outscoring opponents by 24 runs during that span. They decisively defeated the Angels 4-1 yesterday, demonstrating their ability to win by multiple runs. Padres starter Michael King (2-1, 2.78 ERA) also holds a better ERA than Angels starter Reid Detmers (1-1, 3.57 ERA). Given the Padres' hot streak and their recent convincing victory, covering the -1.5 run line is a strong possibility.

picks.game: Kansas City Royals @ New York Yankees
picks.rationale: The New York Yankees hold a superior overall record (12-9) compared to the Kansas City Royals (7-14), and they convincingly beat the Royals 13-4 yesterday. The Royals are in poor form, with a 2-8 record in their last 10 games and a seven-game road losing streak, having been outscored by 21 runs in their last 10. While there is rain in the forecast, if the game proceeds, the Yankees' strong home record (7-5) and recent dominant performance against the Royals give them a good chance to cover the spread.
